document.title: Medical electrical equipment – Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1:2005 third edition specifies general requirements for basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: International medical electrical safety standard used to establish conformity expectations for ME equipment and ME systems, including mandatory requirements, test specifications, risk management, marking, documentation, electrical safety, mechanical safety, PEMS, and EMC topics.
keyPoints: Prepared by IEC subcommittee 62A under IEC technical committee 62.
Cancels and replaces the 1988 second edition and Amendments 1:1991 and 2:1995.
Adds a requirement to include a risk management process and further aligns electrical requirements with IEC 60950-1.
Uses mandatory language: 'shall' means compliance with a requirement or test is mandatory for conformity.
Contains 17 clauses and normative annexes including Annex G and Annex L.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
entities.evidence: IEC is described as a worldwide organization for standardization and the publisher of IEC Publications.

entities.name: IEC subcommittee 62A
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1 was prepared by subcommittee 62A: Common aspects of electrical equipment used in medical practice.

entities.name: IEC technical committee 62
entities.evidence: Subcommittee 62A belongs to IEC technical committee 62: Electrical equipment in medical practice.

entities.name: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
entities.evidence: IEC collaborates closely with ISO under conditions determined by agreement between the two organizations.

entities.name: IEC National Committees
entities.evidence: IEC comprises national electrotechnical committees, and IEC National Committees participate in technical committee work.

concepts.name: Basic safety and essential performance
concepts.evidence: The title states 'General requirements for basic safety and essential performance.'

concepts.name: Medical electrical equipment (ME equipment)
concepts.evidence: The contents list general requirements, testing, classification, identification, marking, documents, and hazard protection for ME equipment.

concepts.name: ME systems
concepts.evidence: Clause 16 is titled 'ME SYSTEMS' and includes general requirements, accompanying documents, power supply, enclosures, separation devices, leakage currents, and mechanical hazards.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: The foreword states that a requirement for including a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS has been added.

concepts.name: Type tests
concepts.evidence: Clause 5 is titled 'General requirements for testing ME EQUIPMENT' and subclause 5.1 is 'TYPE TESTS.'

concepts.name: Applied parts and accessible parts
concepts.evidence: Subclause 5.9 is 'Determination of APPLIED PARTS and ACCESSIBLE PARTS.'

concepts.name: Leakage currents and patient auxiliary currents
concepts.evidence: Subclause 8.7 covers LEAKAGE CURRENTS and PATIENT AUXILIARY CURRENTS; Table 3 gives allowable values under normal and single fault conditions.

concepts.name: Programmable electrical medical systems (PEMS)
concepts.evidence: Clause 14 covers PEMS and includes documentation, risk management plan, development life-cycle, verification, validation, modification, and network/data coupling.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: Clause 17 is titled 'Electromagnetic compatibility of ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.'

concepts.name: Marking and accompanying documents
concepts.evidence: Clause 7 covers ME equipment identification, marking and documents; Annex C guides marking and labelling requirements.

concepts.name: Normative references
concepts.evidence: The foreword states that use of referenced publications is indispensable for correct application of the publication.

concepts.name: IEC approval marking disclaimer
concepts.evidence: IEC states it provides no marking procedure to indicate approval and is not responsible for equipment declared conforming to an IEC Publication.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1:2005 is an International Standard for medical electrical equipment general requirements for basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Cover identifies 'INTERNATIONAL STANDARD 60601-1 Third edition 2005-12' and 'Medical electrical equipment – Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance.'

evidence.claim: The document reference number is CEI/IEC 60601-1:2005.
The standard was prepared by IEC subcommittee 62A under IEC technical committee 62.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states IEC 60601-1 was prepared by subcommittee 62A, Common aspects of electrical equipment used in medical practice, of IEC technical committee 62.

evidence.claim: The third edition cancels and replaces the 1988 second edition and Amendments 1:1991 and 2:1995.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states the third edition cancels and replaces the second edition published in 1988, Amendment 1 (1991), and Amendment 2 (1995).

evidence.claim: The third edition is a technical revision with significant restructuring.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states 'This edition constitutes a technical revision' and 'has been significantly restructured.'

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1:2005 added a requirement to include a risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states that 'a requirement for including a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS has been added.'

evidence.claim: Electrical requirements were further aligned with IEC 60950-1 information technology equipment requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states requirements in the electrical section were further aligned with those for information technology equipment covered by IEC 60950-1.

evidence.claim: Formal approval materials include FDIS 62A/505A/FDIS and report on voting 62A/512/RVD.
evidence.evidenceText: The text of the standard is based on documents: FDIS 62A/505A/FDIS and Report on voting 62A/512/RVD.

evidence.claim: In this standard, 'shall' indicates a mandatory requirement or test for compliance.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword defines 'shall' as meaning compliance with a requirement or test is mandatory for compliance with the standard.

evidence.claim: In this standard, test specifications are printed in italic type and requirements/definitions in roman type.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword lists print types: requirements and definitions in roman type; test specifications in italic type.

evidence.claim: Asterisks on titles indicate related guidance or rationale in Annex A.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states an asterisk as the first character of a title or paragraph/table title indicates guidance or rationale in Annex A.

evidence.claim: IEC provides no approval marking procedure for equipment conformity to IEC Publications.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states IEC provides no marking procedure to indicate approval and cannot be responsible for equipment declared conforming to an IEC Publication.

evidence.claim: Use of normative references is indispensable for correct application of IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states attention is drawn to normative references and their use is indispensable for correct application of the publication.

evidence.claim: Clause 14 addresses programmable electrical medical systems including documentation, risk management, life-cycle, verification, validation, modification, and network/data coupling.
evidence.evidenceText: Contents list Clause 14 'PROGRAMMABLE ELECTRICAL MEDICAL SYSTEMS (PEMS)' with subclauses 14.2 through 14.13 covering these topics.

evidence.claim: Clause 17 addresses electromagnetic compatibility of ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Contents list Clause 17 as 'Electromagnetic compatibility of ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.'

evidence.claim: Annex G is normative and covers protection against ignition hazards of flammable anaesthetic mixtures.
evidence.evidenceText: Contents list 'Annex G (normative) Protection against HAZARDS of ignition of flammable anaesthetic mixtures.'

evidence.claim: Annex L is normative and covers insulated winding wires for use without interleaved insulation.
evidence.evidenceText: Contents list 'Annex L (normative) Insulated winding wires for use without interleaved insulation.'

evidence.claim: The standard includes allowable values and tests related to patient leakage current and patient auxiliary current.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 3 covers allowable values under normal and single fault conditions, and figures show measuring circuits for patient leakage and auxiliary currents.

reviewFlags.reason: This chunk contains front matter, table of contents, figure/table lists, foreword, and the start of the introduction; detailed clause requirements are mostly not present in this chunk.
No specific product model is identified in the chunk; the document applies broadly to medical electrical equipment and ME systems.
